name.markdownDescription:
Logical name for this elastic input

Sets a logical name (e.g., 'myElastic') for this input. The [inputName](https://doc.log10x.com/api/js/#TenXBaseObject+inputName) field returns this value at run time to allow for identifying and operating on instances originating from this input.

enabled.markdownDescription:
Sets whether this input is enabled

Sets whether to open the input stream. To enable this input only when a 'elasticSearchHost' startup argument value is truthy, use: ``` yaml elasticSearchEnabled: $=TenXEnv.get("elasticSearchHost") ``` To learn more see [TenXEnv.get](https://doc.log10x.com/api/js/#TenXEnv.get). (Accepts boolean or string with $= prefix for runtime evaluation) (Default: true)

printProgress.markdownDescription:
Sets whether this input prints throughput stats to the console

Sets whether this input prints throughput stats to the console for testing an integration to a remote endpoint. (Accepts boolean or string with $= prefix for runtime evaluation) (Default: false)

totalDuration.markdownDescription:
Sets maximum time to query the backend

Sets the maximum time the pipeline will continue to query the backend before exiting. For example, setting this to '1min' means that queries to the backend will stop after 1 minute has passed. (Default: 5min)

extractors.markdownDescription:
List of extractors to apply on the events returning from the query

Sets list of extractor names which will work on the events returning from the query. For more info on extractors, see: https://doc.log10x.com/run/input/extract

host.markdownDescription:
Elastic host address

Set the elastic host address to connect to. Examples: coralogix-esapi.coralogix.com api.logz.io <deployment-id>.us-central1.gcp.cloud.es.io

port.markdownDescription:
Elastic server port

Sets the elastic server port to connect to (e.g., '9443') The port value is not needed if [elasticSearchHost](https://doc.log10x.com/run/input/analyzer/elasticsearch/#elasticsearchhost) value encapsulates the port (Accepts number or string with $= prefix for runtime evaluation)

protocol.markdownDescription:
Defines the protocol to connect to ElasticSearch

Sets the protocol to connect to elastic with (e.g., `https`). (Default: https)

token.markdownDescription:
Elastic authentication token

Sets the API token used to authenticate against the target elastic cluster. To learn more see: [api tokens](https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/current/security-api-get-token.html)

authorizationKeyName.markdownDescription:
Prefix for the elastic authentication token

Sets the prefix for the provided [elasticSearchToken](https://doc.log10x.com/run/input/analyzer/elasticsearch/#elasticsearchtoken) when using it in an 'Authorization' header. commonly used values by different elastic vendors are 'ApiKey' and 'Bearer' (Default: ApiKey)

query.markdownDescription:
Query to pass to the elastic server

Sets the elastic query to request paginated documents from the  '/_search' REST endpoint. The default query (defined in 'elasticsearch/query.txt') retrieves paginated values sorted in desc order by @timestamp, starting from the value of 'now()'.

messageField.markdownDescription:
Name of the field used to extract the log message from retrieved documents

Sets the name of the field used to extract the log message from retrieved documents (Default: log)

timestampField.markdownDescription:
Name of the field used to sort retrieved documents by

Sets the name of the field used to sort retrieved documents by (Default: @timestamp)

startTime.markdownDescription:
The start of the time range, expressed as a relative string (i.e., "now-7d") or the number of milliseconds after Jan 1, 1970 00:00:00 UTC.

Sets the start of the time range, expressed as a relative string (i.e., "now-7d") or the number of milliseconds after Jan 1, 1970 00:00:00 UTC. The query includes events with a timestamp equal or later to this value.

endTime.markdownDescription:
The end of the time range, expressed as a relative string (i.e., "now-7d") or the number of milliseconds after Jan 1, 1970 00:00:00 UTC

Sets the end of the time range, expressed as a relative string (i.e., "now-7d") or the number of milliseconds after Jan 1, 1970 00:00:00 UTC. The query excludes events with a timestamp later than this value.

targets.markdownDescription:
List of data streams, indices, and aliases to search

Sets list of data streams, indices, and aliases to search. To search all data streams and indices, omit this parameter or use '*' or '_all'.

endpoint.markdownDescription:
Name of search REST endpoint

Sets the name of the search REST endpoint. (Default: _search)

querySize.markdownDescription:
Number of documents to read per batch from the elastic server

Sets the number of documents to retrieve with each page of results. (Accepts number or string with $= prefix for runtime evaluation) (Default: 500)

retryTimeout.markdownDescription:
A timeout period (in milliseconds) to wait for querying of new data

Determines how long to wait to invoke the search endpoint API again to query for new data. See [elastic-input.js](https://github.com/log-10x/modules/blob/main/pipelines/run/modules/input/analyzer/elasticsearch/elastic-input.js). (Accepts number or string with $= prefix for runtime evaluation) (Default: 10000)

totalBytesLimit.markdownDescription:
Maximum total bytes to read from input before closing

Sets the maximum number of bytes a target pipeline input will read into the pipeline. This value limits the volume of events to read from a local/remote source (e.g., log analyzer).   For example: ``` yaml elasticSearchTotalBytesLimit: $=parseBytes("1GB") ``` (Accepts number or string with $= prefix for runtime evaluation)

totalEventsLimit.markdownDescription:
Maximum total events to read from input before closing

Sets the maximum number of events a target pipeline input will read into the pipeline. This value limits the volume of events to read from a local/remote source (e.g., log analyzer). (Accepts number or string with $= prefix for runtime evaluation) (Default: 10000)

queryInterval.markdownDescription:
Query interval (in milliseconds) for checking new data from remote source

Sets the interval between queries to the remote Elasticsearch API. This controls how frequently the input polls for new log data.  For example: ``` yaml elasticSearchQueryInterval: $=parseDuration("5s") ``` (Accepts number or string with $= prefix for runtime evaluation) (Default: 2000)
